User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the BarExamAI platform.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

registrationDate.description: Date and time the user registered.

Question.title: Question
Question.description: Represents a single bar exam question.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Question entity.

answer.description: The correct answer to the question.

topic.description: The topic or legal area the question relates to.

ExamSession.title: ExamSession
ExamSession.description: Represents a single exam session taken by a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ExamSession entity.

startTime.description: Date and time the exam session started.

endTime.description: Date and time the exam session ended.

score.description: The user's score on the exam session.

questionIds.description: References to Questions in the exam session. (Relationship: ExamSession 1:N Question)

definition.description: Stores user profile information.  Path-based ownership ensures that only the user can access their profile data.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

definition.description: Stores exam session data for each user. Path-based ownership ensures that only the user can access their exam sessions.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: examSessionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the exam session.

definition.description: Stores exam questions. Assumes all questions have the same security requirements (e.g., read access for all users, write access for admins).

params.name: questionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the question.

definition.description: Marks a user as an administrator. Existence of a document in this collection grants admin privileges.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the administrator user.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to provide a secure, scalable, and manageable data model for the BarExamAI application, adhering to the principles of Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and Access Modeling.  It focuses on simplicity and debuggability while staying within the constraints of the Firebase Spark Plan.  

Authorization Independence is achieved by using path-based ownership for user-specific data and avoiding `get()` calls in security rules. For example, `ExamSession` documents are stored under `/users/{userId}/examSessions/{examSessionId}`, ensuring that access control can be determined solely based on the document path and the authenticated user's ID (`request.auth.uid`). There is no dependency on retrieving user data to validate access to `ExamSession` data.  This is also achieved with the admin role, where the mere existence of the `/roles_admin/{userId}` validates admin permissions.

Structural Segregation is maintained by separating user-specific data (like exam sessions) from global data (like questions). Questions are stored in a top-level collection `/questions`, assuming that all questions share the same security requirements (read access for all authenticated users, write access limited to admins).  If different categories of questions required different access control, these would need to be segregated into separate top-level collections.

QAPs (Rules are not Filters) is supported through segregation and path-based ownership.  Listing exam sessions is secure because they are nested under the user's ID. Only the authenticated user can list their own exam sessions.  Global roles are implemented using existence checks, ensuring that only users with a document in the `/roles_admin` collection can perform admin functions. This prevents unauthorized listing of privileged resources.

The structure uses consistent naming conventions and explicit state modeling to enhance clarity and predictability.  For example, `userId` is consistently used to represent user IDs, and timestamps are explicitly named (e.g., `startTime`, `endTime`).
